script:
A few years ago I ordered a late night rideshare after hanging out with some friends.
It was around midnight and the streets were mostly empty.
I got the notification that my driver had arrived, so I walked outside.
A car was waiting at the curb with the same model and color as the one in the app.
I opened the back door and got in.
The driver didn’t say anything, just started driving.
After a minute I tried making small talk and asked how his night was going.
He didn’t respond.
I figured maybe he didn’t speak English or just didn’t feel like talking.
Then I looked down at my phone to check the trip progress.
The app still said my driver was two minutes away.
At first I thought the GPS was glitching.
But then another notification popped up saying, “Your driver has arrived.”
I slowly looked up at the rearview mirror.
The driver was staring at me through it.
Still completely silent.
So I asked, “Uh… are you with the rideshare?”
He shook his head.
Then he said very calmly, “No. You just got in.”
I immediately told him to pull over.
Luckily he did without arguing.
I got out of the car and walked back to the sidewalk.
About thirty seconds later, the actual rideshare car pulled up behind me.
Same model.
Same color.
Same street.
I never found out why the first driver let me in or where he thought we were going.
